Title: Govt rolls out RM60bil drive to strengthen micro, small and medium enterprises
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The government has planned more than 300 development programmes worth RM60 billion this year to help Malaysia's micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) boost growth.
In a statement marking World MSME Day today, Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Minister Steven Sim said the programmes would cover financing, human capital development, market access, infrastructure, as well as technology and innovation.
He said the initiatives are part of the ministry's ABCD framework, which focuses on productivity, simplified bureaucracy, access to capital and market expansion.
"Behind every grocery shop, restaurant, small factory, service provider and family business are millions of Malaysians who depend on micro, small and medium enterprises for their source of income and livelihood," he said.
Sim said micro, small and medium enterprises remain the backbone of Malaysia's economy despite global challenges, including rising operating costs driven by supply chain disruptions and geopolitical uncertainty.
"The sector contributes nearly 40 per cent of Malaysia's gross domestic product (GDP) and employs more than eight million people, accounting for almost half of the country's workforce."
He said government support for micro, small and medium enterprises had yielded positive results last year, with 344 entrepreneurship and development programmes worth RM31.63 billion helping to create 6,600 new entrepreneurs.
Additionally, the initiatives enabled 50,000 MSMEs to improve their business management, while 8,000 digitalised their operations.
He added that government-assisted micro, small and medium enterprises generated RM870 million in additional sales and RM6.8 billion in export sales.
"Our aspiration is not merely to help micro, small and medium enterprises remain in business, but to enable more Malaysian enterprises to grow into high-value companies, expand beyond domestic markets and become creators of value and innovation," he said.
Sim said a stronger micro, small and medium enterprise sector would create more jobs, strengthen the national economy and improve Malaysians' livelihoods.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PH to roll out manifesto focused on federal alignment, development
Article:
KULAI: Pakatan Harapan will soon unveil its Johor election manifesto, focusing on ensuring the state is governed by a coalition aligned with the federal government to secure greater development and benefits.
PH secretariat chief Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the draft manifesto had been finalised but remained subject to final endorsement during a meeting with the coalition's top leadership this Monday.
"It will feature realistic, practical and easily understood pledges for the people, with one of its core pillars highlighting the need for Johor to be administered by a coalition aligned with the federal government to allow the state to enjoy more benefits and development.
"The manifesto this time will feature a framework that is realistic, tangible and reasonable.
"Previously, Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari and I were too idealistic when drafting manifestos, resulting in hundreds of pages covering too many things.
"However, over time, we have become more realistic. A manifesto should be realistic so that the people can understand it and relate to it," he said at a press conference here today.
He said the manifesto would focus on economic stability and prosperity, public welfare, political stability, good governance, increasing state revenue, and fair distribution to the people.
He said PH would emphasise performance-based politics over identity politics, which he described as divisive.
"There are no winners in identity politics. Instead, it only creates tension. That is why we want to offer performance-based politics and solutions to the people," he said.
Undoubtedly, the manifesto will cover all matters, presented in a simpler format without compromising our offerings, he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar to launch LRT3 Shah Alam Line tomorrow
Article:
SHAH ALAM: The Light Rail Transit 3 (LRT3) Shah Alam Line, spanning 37.8 kilometres from Bandar Utama to Johan Setia, has been fully completed and is ready for passenger service.
Shah Alam Line chief Mohd Ariffin Idris confirmed that the official launch, expected to benefit some two million residents along its corridor, will be officiated by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim at a ceremony on Sunday, June 28.
He added that the prime minister will announce the commercial opening date and time for the 20-station route during the event.
"The new stations are Bandar Utama, Kayu Ara, BU 11 (Bandar Utama 11), Damansara Idaman, Subang, Glenmarie 2, Kerjaya, Stadium Shah Alam, Dato' Menteri and UiTM Shah Alam.
"The remaining 10 stations are Seksyen 7 Shah Alam, Bandar Baru Klang, Pasar Klang, Jalan Meru, Jambatan Kota, Taman Selatan, Seri Andalas, Klang Jaya, Bandar Bukit Tinggi and Johan Setia," he told reporters during a pre-opening media ride on the new service.
Passengers will enjoy seamless transfers, Mohd Ariffin explained, with connections to the Kajang Line at Bandar Utama and to the Kelana Jaya Line at Glenmarie 2.
To make commuting even easier, the network is supported by 40 feeder buses operating across 13 routes with 323 stops, all for RM1 per ride, from 6am to 11.30pm.
"We also offer Rapid On-Demand van services, with 44 vans serving 20 zones at RM2 per trip, available during the same operating hours," he said.
He added that both feeder bus routes and van services will be fine-tuned based on commuter demand and travel patterns once the Shah Alam Line is operational.
For those driving, parking is available at Kayu Ara, Damansara Idaman, Pasar Besar Klang, Sri Andalas, Bandar Bukit Tinggi and Johan Setia stations, with a total of 2,300 bays.
Looking ahead, Mohd Ariffin said that Prasarana Malaysia Berhad (Prasarana) is targeting 67,000 daily passengers in the first year, with that figure expected to rise to 117,708 per day within five years.
The Shah Alam Line is also embracing sustainability, he said, with green features such as inverter-based air conditioning for light rail vehicles and smart cooling systems.
Station designs have been carefully planned to maximise natural airflow and daylight, reducing energy use.
Accessibility is another key priority, with all stations equipped with ramps for wheelchair users, disability-compliant toilets, and reserved seating for persons with disabilities in every carriage.
As for the Tropicana, Raja Muda, Temasya, Bukit Raja and Bandar Botanik stations slated for reconstruction, Mohd Ariffin confirmed that work will begin by the end of this year. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Five-cornered fights set to shake up Bukit Batu, Puteri Wangsa, Bukit Naning
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Three key Johor state seats are set for five-cornered contests, setting the stage for unpredictable outcomes in Bukit Batu, Puteri Wangsa and Bukit Naning as rival blocs vie for fragmented voter support.
The Election Commission confirmed that all three constituencies will feature five candidates, raising the prospect of vote-splitting in closely watched urban and semi-urban seats where winning margins have traditionally been narrow.
In Bukit Batu, incumbent Pakatan Harapan assemblyman Arthur Chiong Sen Sern will defend his seat against Barisan Nasional's R. Kumaran, Muda's M. Premanand, Bersama's G. Tamili and independent Datuk Kamaruzaman Ali.
In the 2022 state election, Chiong won the seat in a four-cornered contest with a razor-thin majority of 137 votes after polling 9,439 votes.
In Puteri Wangsa, PH's Dr Maszlee Malik will face BN's Teow Chia Ling, Muda's Rashifa Aljunied, Bersama's Nicholas Paul Vincent and independent Wang Wee Siong.
With 112,804 voters, Puteri Wangsa has the second-largest electorate in Johor after Skudai, which has 128,723 registered eligible voters.
The seat, under the Tebrau parliamentary constituency, also witnessed a crowded contest in the 2022 election.
Muda's Amira Aisya won the six-cornered contest with 22,884 votes and a 7,114-vote majority over BN's Ng Yew Aik.
PN's Loh Kah Yong polled 8,957 votes, Parti Bangsa Malaysia's Steven Choong secured 2,471, Pejuang's Khairil Anwar Razali garnered 2,468, while independent Adzrin Adam obtained 398.
The constituency also has a relatively young electorate, with 11,573 voters aged between 18 and 20, while those aged 21 to 29 form the largest age group at 32,776.
Bukit Naning mirrors the same pattern, with BN, PH, PN, Bersama and an independent candidate locked in another five-cornered contest.
Bukit Naning incumbent Datuk Dr Mohd Fuad Tukirin was dropped by BN and replaced with Mohd Ghazali Sabari.
Mohd Ghazali will face PH's Md Ysahrudin Kusni, Bersama's Iskandar Md Alias, PN's Mohd Radzi Amin and independent S Jeganathan.
The five-cornered contests in all three seats are expected to heighten electoral uncertainty, with disciplined grassroots machinery and voter turnout likely to prove more decisive than popularity alone.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 9 arrested in illegal bauxite mining raid, RM3.8mil haul seized in Kuantan
Article:
KUANTAN: Authorities arrested nine people and seized about 10,000 tonnes of bauxite-bearing soil, heavy machinery and lorries worth a combined RM3.75 million in a raid on an illegal mining operation at a Felda plantation in Bukit Goh.
According to Harian Metro, the raid at 11.30am on June 25 under Op Bersepadu Khazanah involved the General Operations Force (GOF) Battalion 7 Intelligence Branch and the State Enforcement Unit.
Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the seizure included soil suspected to contain bauxite worth RM2 million, two excavators valued at RM1 million and three lorries worth RM750,000.
Preliminary investigations found that five of the nine suspects were foreign nationals without valid identification documents or work permits.
Checks also revealed the excavation was carried out without the necessary licences and approvals from the local authority and state government.
Investigations showed the land belonged to a Felda settler and had been leased to a company for land-clearing and oil palm replanting works.
"The excavated soil was transported to a processing plant in Gebeng before being sent to buyers," Ahmad Radzi said.
The four Indonesians and one Bangladeshi who were detained were handed over to police for further investigation, while the remaining suspects and seized items were handed over to the State Enforcement Unit, which also ordered an immediate halt to operations at the site.
The case is being investigated under Section 158 of the Pahang State Mineral Enactment 2001 for illegal mineral extraction, as well as Sections 15(1)(c), 39(b) and 6(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63 for immigration-related offences
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police tracking individual believed to have been with missing Nor Afiza
Article:
TAIPING: Police are tracking an individual believed to have been with 41-year-old Nor Afiza Zainuddin before her disappearance 11 days ago.
Acting Taiping police chief Superintendent Mohd Ariffridzuan Ezahar said the search for the mother of two was ongoing, with investigators pursuing fresh leads.
"We have not located her yet. We are also tracking a person believed to have been with her before she went missing. We have recorded statements from her family members and close friends," he said when contacted today.
Nor Afiza was reported missing on June 18. She was last seen leaving her mother's house in a white Perodua Axia, reportedly to attend a job interview at the Casuarina Hotel in Kuala Kangsar.
Ariffridzuan said investigations revealed that she did not attend the interview.
Her vehicle was found on June 21 at the Kampung Paya Lintang recreational site in Batu Hampar, near Padang Rengas. Police said the car was in good condition, with the keys and several of her personal belongings still inside.
Nor Afiza is described as 155cm tall, weighing 57kg, with a tanned complexion, a flat nose, and wearing glasses.
Anyone with information is urged to contact their nearest police station, investigating officer Inspector Sanjay Shanmugam at 012-5039820, or the Taiping police headquarters at 05-8291222.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PM: Malaysia must accelerate adoption of drone technology
Article:
SEPANG: Malaysia must accelerate its adoption and development of new technologies, particularly in the Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) or drone industry, to bolster its global economic standing, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
He said the drone industry, which intersects with artificial intelligence (AI) and digital transformation, has emerged as a powerful catalyst for innovation, productivity and economic growth.
"If Malaysia wants to become a more successful country in terms of its economy, it must be fast in developing new technologies and fields.
"Drones are related to digital technology and AI, and we need to build an ecosystem that is suitable and reasonable for them," he said at the MyDrone Expo 2026 (MDX2026) closing ceremony here today.
Co-organised by the World UAV Federation (WUAVF) Malaysia Chapter and the Malaysia UAV Development Association (MUDA), the three-day expo surpassed expectations by featuring more than 100 exhibition booths and attracting thousands of visitors and industry delegates from 46 countries.
Anwar pointed out that the global UAV market is projected to exceed USD55 billion by 2030, while the low-altitude economy is becoming an increasingly important driver of investment.
To seize these opportunities, Anwar has directed Cabinet ministers, policymakers, science research establishments and all related agencies to extend full regulatory and ecosystem support to the industry.
"The policy is clear. Drones and the related industry are given full support, not only for the air force and defence industry but also for civil activities, such as plantations, to enhance productivity," he added.
To further drive the industry, Anwar said the government is committed to improving the regulatory framework, enhancing research funding, testing and certification.
He also urged closer collaboration between private players, government research establishments and universities to build a robust talent pipeline, similar to existing faculties in AI and quantum computing. – BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Media Prima recognised for role in nationwide anti-drug awareness
Article:
KEPALA BATAS: Media Prima Bhd was today recognised by the National Anti-Drug Agency (Nada) for playing a key role in nationwide anti-drug awareness campaigns.
Penang Yang di-Pertua Negeri Tun Ramli Ngah Talib presented Media Prima OMNIA chief operating officer Datuk Mohd Efendi Omar with a plaque in recognition of the company's contribution to drug prevention during the National Anti-Drug Day 2026 celebration here.
Media Prima was also appointed Nada's strategic media partner, becoming the first media company to hold the role.
Nada director-general Datuk Ruslin Jusoh said Media Prima's extensive reach across television, radio, print and digital platforms made it an ideal partner in strengthening drug prevention efforts.
"We see Media Prima playing a prominent role in drug prevention programmes through its television channels, radio stations, newspapers and digital platforms.
"With its large audience, Media Prima can help bring anti-drug awareness messages directly to the people. This collaboration will provide fresh impetus to our prevention efforts," he said.
Ruslin said Nada hoped the collaboration would continue through more joint programmes and corporate social responsibility (CSR) initiatives in support of its goal of achieving a drug-controlled Malaysia by 2030.
He also acknowledged the continued support of Media Prima's print publications - the New Straits Times, Berita Harian and Harian Metro - for their consistent coverage of the agency's programmes and initiatives.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid does not rule out 'lower-level leader' talks between BN, Pas and Wawasan
Article:
SIMPANG RENGGAM: Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has not ruled out the possibility of internal discussions on cooperation involving Pas and Parti Wawasan Negara (Wawasan) in the Johor election.
However, he said such discussions, if they had taken place, were likely held at lower levels within the party.
Zahid, who is also Deputy Prime Minister, said BN remained focused on contesting all 56 seats under its own banner.
"I did not hear about that matter. It may have been discussed at lower levels. The fact is, as stated earlier by state BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz, we wanted to go solo.
"And it is proven that we are contesting all 56 seats in the state legislative assembly with BN candidates, and not a single one of our candidates is using another party's symbol," he told reporters this after a lunch meeting with BN machinery for the Benut state seat here today.
When asked to clarify whether BN was denying the alleged arrangement, Zahid said such discussions, if any, could have taken place at the third or fourth tier of party leadership.
"In politics, there are always discussions on various possibilities. But if those possibilities do not materialise, then we stick to contesting solo."
Wawasan, previously known as Parti Cinta Malaysia, has been rebranded and is now part of a political cooperation under Perikatan Nasional (PN), alongside Pas and other component parties.
Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Lokman Noor Adam has previously confirmed that there would be renewed cooperation with Pas in the state election, despite earlier saying he did not want to be "bitten by the same snake twice".
He said it was a second chance for Umno to re-establish cooperation with Pas, with the possibility of including Wawasan.
Lokman was also quoted as saying that Umno's main concern was no longer cooperation with Pas per se, but the possibility of parties abandoning coalitions midway after elections.
He said one measure being considered to strengthen cooperation was the use of the BN logo, which would bind all candidates under a single agreement.
He added that such an agreement would empower the BN chairman to negotiate and decide on political cooperation and the formation of state governments after the election.
On June 8, Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man was reported as saying that talks on cooperation between the Islamist party and Umno — facilitated through an internal committee involving both parties — had yet to produce any positive outcome.
He said claims that Pas had been offered exco posts and other appointments by Umno, particularly ahead of the Johor, Negri Sembilan and Melaka elections, were merely rumours.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Elevating Syariah court status won't affect non-Muslims' rights, says PM
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Efforts to strengthen the Syariah court system will not infringe on the rights of non-Muslims to freely practise their religion, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
Anwar said the government remained committed to enhancing the status of the Syariah courts while safeguarding the rights of non-Muslims and addressing their concerns.
"There may be minor concerns, but this is not intended to deny them the right to practise their religion.
"We must, however, elevate the dignity and standing of the Syariah courts, in line with our recognition as a nation where Islam is the religion of the Federation.
"In my three years as Prime Minister, not a single proposal, including those aimed at strengthening the Syariah courts, has been disputed or objected to by any minister, including non-Muslim ministers," he said here today.
Anwar said Putrajaya was actively addressing structural and systemic gaps in the Syariah justice system.
"The political will is clear, as is our commitment to finding the best way to elevate the status of the Syariah courts.
"At the same time, we must build confidence by ensuring implementation is clear and orderly while reassuring non-Muslims so that it does not create unnecessary apprehension," he said in his keynote address at the 2026 Syariah Lawyer National Convention.
He said discussions were ongoing with the Attorney-General, Syariah court judges and legal practitioners to modernise the institution, particularly since Article 121(1A) was introduced into the Federal Constitution in 1988.
"I strive to explain these matters rationally. Everyone has the right to understand issues relating to Islamic law.
"What is the issue with non-Muslims knowing? There is a view that says, 'This cannot be done because PMX is afraid,' or 'This is an Islamic matter, so it should not be discussed.' I do not agree.
"The final decision rests with Muslims, but all Malaysians have the right to know what is being amended or introduced within the division of powers, and whether it affects their rights.
"Our approach must be to provide clear explanations. We explain Islam, non-Muslims may ask questions, and we provide the answers," Anwar said.
He acknowledged that introducing Islamic reforms could face resistance, not only from non-Muslims but also from within the Muslim community because of historical secular influences.
As an example of the government's balanced approach, Anwar cited the gazetting of 50 acres of prime land in Bandar Malaysia as Malay Reserve Land to protect the rights of the Malay and Muslim communities.
At the event, Anwar also announced a RM1.5 million allocation for Persatuan Peguam Syarie Malaysia to fund the Syariah Legal Aid Programme, ensuring that those in need have access to justice.
"Usually, those who are well-off can afford the best lawyers, but the poorest sometimes have no access at all.
"Therefore, those in need, especially single mothers who require legal representation or defence, must be assisted," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BN eyes higher seat count in Johor election, says Zahid
Article:
SIMPANG RENGGAM: Barisan Nasional (BN) is targeting an increase in the number of seats in the Johor election compared with the previous polls, its chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said.
Zahid, who is also Umno president, however, stopped short of revealing BN's target for the July 11 election.
"We have placed candidates across all constituencies, so of course we have certain targets. We have various structures and work mechanisms, and we certainly want to achieve more.
"However, it depends on how the machinery, whether media or social media, and ground teams respond to issues raised by opponents in each constituency," he said.
He said this after a lunch meeting with BN machinery for the Benut state seat here today.
In the previous state election, BN secured a two-thirds majority, winning 40 of the 56 state seats.
Pakatan Harapan (PH) won 12 seats, followed by Perikatan Nasional (PN) with three, while Muda secured one seat.
Zahid, who is also Deputy Prime Minister, said the target for additional seats was supported by the performance of caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi in leading the state administration.
"Onn Hafiz is a hardworking leader. It is not only about development, allocations and state revenue growth, but also about how those allocations are distributed inclusively.
"I have seen in the manifesto presented to me that no district in Johor will be left behind.
"This gives an advantage to the people of Johor, and if voters are influenced by various issues being played up, it would be a loss if they do not give Onn Hafiz another opportunity to continue his service," he said.
He also urged BN machinery not to attack political opponents such as PH, despite being on different sides in the state contest.
"We are friends and we are competing in the same arena with our partners in the unity government at the federal level.
"However, we have agreed that we should not attack opponents even if we differ in views in Johor.
"We must look at the bigger national picture, and this is well understood by the leadership within the unity government."
Johor goes to the polls on July 11, with early voting scheduled for July 7.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pakistani man held for allegedly posing as doctor in Selangor clinic
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Immigration Department has detained a Pakistani man suspected of posing as a doctor at a private clinic in Subang Bestari, Selangor, in a crackdown on illegal medical practice involving foreigners.
The suspect was arrested during a special operation on Wednesday by officers from the department's Intelligence and Special Operations Division, following a month-long intelligence-gathering exercise.
In a Facebook post, the department said initial checks found the man did not possess a valid pass or travel documents to remain in Malaysia.
The arrest marks the fourth case since 2024 involving foreign nationals allegedly impersonating or working as unlicensed doctors in the country.
During the raid, enforcement officers seized medical equipment believed to have been used in the illegal practice, along with the suspect's name stamp, a clinic stamp, tablets, business records, an Annual Practising Certificate, and documents from the Companies Commission of Malaysia.
"Preliminary investigations revealed that the suspect had allegedly been using the premises of a private clinic to provide medical services illegally.
"The clinic is also believed to have been operated by a foreign national without authorisation to work in Malaysia's medical sector.
"Investigators believe the suspect had been employed by the individual to perform the duties of a doctor despite lacking the necessary qualifications.
"To deceive the authorities and patients, the clinic allegedly used another person's practising certificate and medical credentials," it said.
The department also believes the clinic issued medical certificates without conducting any medical examination or health assessment, charging RM20 for each certificate.
The clinic is believed to have been operating for the past eight months.
The suspect is being investigated under Section 6(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63 for remaining in Malaysia without valid travel documents.
A local woman has also been issued a notice to appear before the Immigration Department to assist in investigations.
The department said it would continue firm action against activities that violate the country's laws to safeguard national security.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Muhyiddin confident PN has chance to form Johor government despite contesting 33 seats
Article:
PAGOH: Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin is confident that there is still a chance for Perikatan Nasional (PN) to form the next state government despite the opposition coalition contesting only 33 seats in the Johor election.
He said there were allies outside PN, such as Muda, which were willing to form an electoral pact with the opposition.
"There are also other partners who are willing to work with us to form an electoral pact, for example, Muda.
"As such, do not underestimate us. There is still a chance (for PN to form the next state government).
"Numbers are one thing. What matters is the choice of the voters, and they know what we are fighting for," he told reporters.
Muhyiddin said this when asked to comment on Johor Umno Youth chief Noor Azleen Ambros, who had questioned PN's commitment to forming the next state government based on the number of seats the opposition was contesting.
A total of 56 seats are up for grabs in the state election. PN needs to secure 29 seats to achieve a simple majority to form the next state government.
Observers, however, believe PN's chances are dim, taking into consideration the fallout between Bersatu and Pas, which has escalated over the past few months.
The rift widened after Pas recently announced its decision to sever political cooperation with Bersatu. Both parties, however, decided to remain in PN.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Same village, different flags: Harmonious contest expected in Semerah
Article:
BATU PAHAT: The contest for Semerah is shaping up to be one of the Johor election's most cordial, with all three candidates hailing from the same village.
"We're all from Semerah, from the same village," said Barisan Nasional candidate Mohd Fared Khalid.
Fared will defend the seat against Pakatan Harapan's Khuzzan Abu Bakar and Perikatan Nasional's Halim Kepol.
"I can assure you the contest in Semerah will be one of the most harmonious.
"We don't know the meaning of quarrelling. There has always been a strong sense of brotherhood and consensus in Semerah," he said after the announcement of candidates.
Fared said he would rely on his track record of service in seeking another term.
"Even during the campaign, we will continue explaining matters related to the people's welfare. The voters will judge the work we have done throughout my tenure as assemblyman," he said.
Meanwhile, Khuzzan, a former Johor executive councillor, said he would draw on his experience in his bid to return to the state assembly.
"I will use my experience as a former assemblyman and exco member to my advantage. I will prove to the people of Semerah that I am capable of serving them," he said.
Pakatan Harapan communications director Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, who accompanied Khuzzan on nomination day, urged him to run a clean, fact-based campaign.
Fahmi, who is also communications minister, said the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) would operate round the clock throughout the campaign period to monitor social media content.
"Our goal is to curb harmful content, including fake news and material touching on the 3R issues of race, religion and the Rulers," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Drug suspects arrested after high-speed chase, crash in KL
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Two suspects allegedly transporting drugs in a Perodua Viva led police on a high-speed chase before crashing into three other vehicles in a bid to escape along Jalan Kuching here yesterday.
Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said both suspects were remanded today to assist investigations.
He said the incident unfolded at 5.30pm when the Wangsa Maju narcotics investigation team spotted the vehicle in Wangsa Maju.
The driver refused to stop when ordered by police, triggering a pursuit that ended at Jalan Kuching.
"Upon arriving at the scene, the driver of the Perodua Viva drove recklessly, weaving between lanes before losing control and slamming into three vehicles caught in a traffic jam," he said in a statement today.
Both suspects, aged 40 and 42, were pinned down at the scene. A search of the vehicle uncovered 19.63g of suspected heroin.
The case is being investigated under Section 39B of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952, while the crash is being investigated under Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987.
Section 39B of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952 carries the death penalty or life imprisonment for drug trafficking.
Fadil said the pile-up involved the suspects' Perodua Viva, another Perodua Viva driven by a 60-year-old man, a Perodua Bezza driven by a 42-year-old man, and a Honda City driven by a 20-year-old woman.
He said there were no injuries or fatalities, with only vehicle damage reported.
Those with information on the incident are urged to contact the Sentul police hotline at 03-4048 2222, the Kuala Lumpur police hotline at 03-2115 9999, the Jalan Tun H.S. Lee traffic police station at 03-2071 9999, or any nearby police station.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Youths make up 84pct of synthetic drug users
Article:
KEPALA BATAS: Youths aged between 15 and 39 account for 84 per cent of Malaysia's synthetic drug users, with the highest abuse rates recorded in east coast states.
National Anti-Drug Agency director-general Datuk Ruslin Jusoh said the trend reflected the growing dominance of synthetic drugs, which now accounted for 73 per cent of all drug abuse cases nationwide.
"The worrying trend is that 84 per cent of synthetic drug users are youths aged between 15 and 39, and the figure continues to rise based on our close monitoring," he told reporters after the National Anti-Drug Day 2026 celebration here today.
Ruslin said east coast states continued to record the highest rates of synthetic drug abuse, particularly involving yaba pills and syabu, although cases were also rising in west coast states.
"In the east coast, yaba pills and syabu remain the drugs of choice, while the abuse of traditional drugs such as heroin and cannabis is much lower.
"However, we cannot deny that synthetic drug abuse is also high in the west coast states," he added.
Ruslin attributed the growing prevalence of synthetic drug abuse among youths to peer influence, easy availability and the faster intoxicating effects compared with traditional drugs.
"We are concerned that synthetic drug use is becoming part of the lifestyle among some youths.
"They can obtain these drugs easily and experience the effects much faster than with traditional drugs," he said.
Ruslin said personal and family issues also contributed to drug abuse, with some youths turning to synthetic drugs to cope with stress, relationship problems or domestic conflicts.
He said the rise in synthetic drug abuse had created new challenges for the drug agency, forcing the agency to strengthen its treatment and rehabilitation modules, originally designed for traditional drugs such as heroin, morphine and cannabis.
"We have to continuously improve our rehabilitation modules because synthetic drugs present a different challenge. We are also seeing new forms of abuse, including synthetic drugs mixed into vape liquids," he said.
He said the changing drug landscape underscored the need for stronger prevention efforts, particularly among young people, to curb the growing appeal of synthetic drugs.
It was reported that Malaysia's drug landscape has shifted decisively towards synthetic drugs, which now account for nearly three-quarters of all abuse cases, driving the government to enhance artificial intelligence-powered enforcement.
Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said amphetamine-type stimulants, particularly methamphetamine (syabu), ecstasy and yaba pills, continued to
dominate drug abuse nationwide
.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesian police arrest dozens in anti-government protest [WATCH]
Article:
SURABAYA: Indonesian police have arrested dozens of protesters following a rally against President Prabowo Subianto's policies in the country's second-largest city, a rights group said on Saturday.
Around 100 people gathered near a government building in Surabaya on Friday, AFP journalists estimated, to protest a fuel price hike and Prabowo's flagship free meals scheme.
Some demonstrators hurled rocks towards police and set fire to rubbish in the middle of the road, prompting officers to arrest them, the reporters saw.
Twenty-four protesters were arrested, Fatkul Khoir, a coordinator at rights group KontraS Surabaya, told AFP on Saturday.
Police questioned them until 3.30am on Saturday but did not charge them, he said.
Surabaya police chief Luthfie Sulistiawan told reporters late Friday that officers had taken "firm actions" to push back protesters after some threw projectiles.
He said there had been "dozens" of arrests but did not specify the number.
Protests erupted in several Indonesian cities this month after Prabowo's administration raised prices of non-subsidised gasoline by around 30 per cent, seeking to ease budgetary pressures as the Middle East war weighs on global oil supplies.
Thousands of students took to the streets in the capital Jakarta to oppose both the fuel price hikes and to demand a halt to the multi-billion-dollar free meals scheme.
The initiative has been a cornerstone of Prabowo's political agenda but has received widespread criticism for being wasteful and linked to mass food poisonings. It has since been partly suspended.
Indonesia's biggest unrest since Prabowo took office in 2024 came in August and September, when nationwide protests took place initially over lavish perks for lawmakers.
However, they spiralled into an outburst of anger against security forces after a police vehicle ran over a delivery driver and killed him.
Rights groups said those protests resulted in 10 deaths and thousands of arrests, though most people have since been released.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: Maszlee says PH win will usher in new chapter for Johor
Article:
JOHOR BARU: A win for Pakatan Harapan at Puteri Wangsa and other seats in the Johor election will usher in a new chapter for the development of the state, former education minister Dr Maszlee Malik said.
Maszlee is the PH candidate for Puteri Wangsa, which is one of the three seats that will see a five-cornered fight in the July 11 polls.
"Victory in Puteri Wangsa and across the state will usher in a new chapter for Johor," he told reporters after the nomination process for the seat concluded today.
Maszlee said he wants to introduce balanced development and progress in the state constituency in the event he wins the election.
He added that his vision also includes boosting investments that would create more job opportunities.
"We are determined to bring Malaysians working abroad back to Johor to work here.
"In addition, we will ensure education remains a key agenda, while the welfare of persons with disabilities (PWDs) in Johor is given due attention.
"Young people should have the opportunity to buy homes at affordable prices, while more community childcare centres and workplace nurseries will be provided to ease the burden on working women," he said.
Maszlee said that as part of efforts to increase voter turnout, his team would mount a strong campaign to bring back Johor voters.
"Although polling day falls on a Saturday, we will try to appeal to voters, including those working in Singapore and Kuala Lumpur, to return home to vote.
"Voters are not only voting for a winning party, but for their future and the future of Johor," he said.
Maszlee is up against four candidates in the race for Puteri Wangsa. They include Muda's Rashifa Aljunied, Barisan Nasional candidate Teow Chia Ling, Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) candidate Nicholas Paul Vincent, and independent candidate Wang Wee Siong.
In the 2022 state election, Muda, through Amira Aisya Abdul Aziz, who is now the party president, won the seat after she polled 22,884, defeating five other contenders.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Synthetic drug abuse fuels rise in mental health disorders among addicts
Article:
KEPALA BATAS: Synthetic drug abuse is fuelling a sharp rise in mental health disorders among addicts, posing a growing challenge to rehabilitation efforts.
National Anti-Drug Agency director-general Datuk Ruslin Jusoh said the agency was seeing an alarming increase in "dual diagnosis" cases, where addicts were also diagnosed with psychiatric disorders.
"From January to March this year, 1,346 people, or 27 per cent of the 5,030 drug addicts and substance abusers undergoing institutional treatment and rehabilitation programmes at Narcotics Addiction Rehabilitation Centres, were found to have dual diagnosis."
"For the corresponding period in 2025, only 548 individuals, or six per cent of the 9,361 addicts and substance abusers undergoing the same rehabilitation programmes, were diagnosed with dual diagnosis," he told reporters after the National Anti-Drug Day 2026 celebration here today.
Ruslin said the figures pointed to a worrying rise in cases involving both addiction and mental illness, complicating treatment and rehabilitation.
"This is no longer just about drug addiction. We are now dealing with addicts who are also suffering from mental health disorders, and this has become a major challenge because our core business has traditionally focused on treating substance dependence," he added.
He said most of the mental health conditions were linked to synthetic drug abuse, particularly amphetamine-type stimulants such as syabu, yaba pills and ecstasy.
According to the drug agency, synthetic drugs now account for more than 73 per cent of all drug abuse cases in the country, while youths aged between 15 and 39 make up 84 per cent of users.
Ruslin said patients with dual diagnosis displayed symptoms ranging from depression and emotional instability to hallucinations, with some showing tendencies towards self-harm or suicide attempts.
"Some experience hallucinations severe enough to drive them towards self-harm or suicidal behaviour. This is why these cases require more specialised intervention than conventional addiction treatment," he said.
Ruslin said the drug agency was strengthening its rehabilitation modules to address the changing drug landscape and was working closely with the Health Ministry to provide integrated treatment for addiction and mental health conditions.
He attributed the growing abuse of synthetic drugs among youths to peer influence, easier access, the drugs' rapid intoxicating effects, and personal or family problems.
Ruslin said the emergence of synthetic drugs, including those mixed into vape liquids, had changed the nature of drug addiction in Malaysia, requiring rehabilitation programmes to evolve alongside new drug trends.
"The challenge today is very different from the past. We must continuously improve our treatment approaches because synthetic drugs have changed both the profile of addicts and the complexity of their recovery," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PH faces four-cornered fight to retain Skudai
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The battle for Skudai has developed into a four-cornered contest involving Barisan Nasional, Pakatan Harapan, Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) and Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM) candidates.
The seat will see a four-way fight involving BN's Nicol Tan, PH's J. Kartiyaini, Bersama's Eugene Chua and PSM's Amir Syafiq Ameer Soekre.
The BN and PH candidates launched their campaigns by outlining contrasting priorities for one of Johor's closely watched constituencies.
Nicol, 43, said restoring dignity to residents through better housing would be her first priority if elected.
"The housing in several parts of this area is in a poor state, and I hope to improve the living conditions of the people here," she said after filing her nomination papers at the N48 Skudai nomination centre at the Iskandar Puteri City Council hall.
The MCA National Women's Central Committee member said her relocation from Perling, where she contested in the 2022 state election, would not affect her commitment to serving constituents.
"It makes no difference where I contest. What matters is earning the people's trust and bringing their voices to the Johor legislative assembly," she said.
Meanwhile, Kartiyaini, 37, said she was determined to retain the seat for PH despite making her electoral debut and succeeding the former assemblyman Marina Ibrahim, who opted not to seek re-election.
The Johor DAP assistant organising secretary said her lifelong ties to Skudai gave her a clear understanding of the community's needs.
"I believe the party selected me because it trusts that I am the right person for Skudai. I was born and raised here, I still live here, and my parents continue to reside here.
"I have served in politics for more than a decade and understand the challenges faced by the community. Skudai needs holistic development, and I will do my best to serve its residents," she said.
In the 2022 Johor election, Marina won the Skudai seat with 26,359 votes and a majority of 13,943 in a three-cornered contest.
On May 31, Marina became the first incumbent lawmaker to bow out, after she announced that she
would not be defending
her seat.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Foreigner who threw brick at police car remanded for 14 days
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A foreigner who threw a brick at a police car in front of a hotel in Jalan Hang Lekiu has been remanded for another 14 days.
Dang Wangi deputy police chief Superintendent Nuzulan Mohd Din said the suspect, a vagrant in his 40s, is being remanded to facilitate investigations under the Immigration Act.
"He will be held until July 10," he said when contacted.
It was reported yesterday that the suspect, who is believed to have health issues, was arrested after damaging a police car in front of the hotel.
Nuzulan said the suspect was detained at the scene with the help of the public at 11.57pm.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Public backs Penang's community service order for litterbugs
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Penang's community service order for litterbugs has received strong public support ahead of its July 1 enforcement, with residents, traders and tourists backing the measure.
State Local Government and Town and Country Planning Committee chairman H'ng Mooi Lye said feedback gathered during a public awareness campaign in Lebuh Armenian showed broad support for the initiative.
He said the measure was aimed at promoting cleaner habits and strengthening civic responsibility.
"They view the implementation of the community service order as an
effective measure to strengthen
the culture of cleanliness and foster greater civic responsibility," he said today.
H'ng said the state government hoped the initiative would encourage the public to take greater responsibility for keeping public spaces clean and help maintain Penang as a clean, attractive and comfortable destination.
The awareness campaign saw H'ng, Penang Island City Council  mayor Datuk A. Rajendran, councillors and senior officials distributing leaflets to residents, traders and tourists ahead of the implementation of the Community Service Order.
From July 1, local authorities statewide will begin enforcing the measure against littering offences.
The offences include discarding cigarette butts, tissues, plastic bags, bottles and food wrappers in public places, throwing rubbish into drains, leaving litter in parks, beaches and recreational areas, disposing of rubbish from vehicles, and abandoning food waste after trading in public spaces.
H'ng said each operation would be led by an operations officer, while enforcement personnel would wear body cameras to ensure transparency, professionalism and provide evidence.
He said enforcement officers could issue a Notice of Offence  before referring cases to court.
Upon conviction, offenders may be fined up to RM2,000, ordered to perform up to 12 hours of community service within six months, or both.
Those who fail to comply with a court-ordered community service sentence may face fines of between RM2,000 and RM10,000 under existing laws.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: Muhyiddin says 'it's alright' after Pas machinery snub
Article:
PAGOH: Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin appeared unfazed by Pas' decision not to deploy its election machinery at seats contested by Bersatu candidates in the state election.
The Bersatu president, who is also Pagoh member of parliament, said he accepted the decision made by Pas despite the two parties contesting under Perikatan Nasional in the July 11 polls.
"It is alright. I am not asking who wants to help. If anyone wants to help, then help.
"This is although, in principle, there is supposed to be mutual assistance among PN members.
"However, if there are partners who do not want to help, then we accept it. So far, we have been working hard based on our own strengths, policies and practices, and I am confident voters will know how to make their decision," he said after accompanying the PN candidate for Bukit Kepong, Datuk Dr Sahruddin Jamal, during nomination day for the polls today.
Asked if Bersatu is ready to assist PN candidates, including those from Pas, during campaigning for the election, Muhyiddin said: "If they want our support, we will help.
"Our practice is one of openness."
Earlier today, it was reported that Pas had instructed its election machinery assigned to
Bersatu-contested seats
in Johor to instead be deployed to constituencies contested by other PN component parties.
The party's information chief, Ahmad Fadhli Shaari, said in a Facebook post that the directive applies to both divisional and state constituency-level machinery.
Yesterday, Utusan Malaysia quoted Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang as saying that Pas and Bersatu would operate their election machinery separately despite contesting under the PN banner.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Heritage festival spotlights revival of KL Railway Station
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Railway Assets Corporation (RAC) is holding The Heritage Colours Festival at the Kuala Lumpur Railway Station from June 26-28 as part of efforts to promote appreciation of one of Malaysia's best-known heritage buildings and encourage greater public engagement with the site.
The three-day festival, held daily from 10am to 6pm, aims to showcase the station's historical, architectural and cultural significance while reviving the surrounding area as a more inclusive and community-oriented public space.
The festival's opening day also featured the second edition of Jelajah Warisan KL, a stakeholder engagement programme that reviewed ongoing conservation and activation efforts around the Kuala Lumpur Railway Station heritage precinct.
The programme, held under the Warisan KL initiative, was officiated by Kuala Lumpur mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud and attended by senior representatives from RAC, the Warisan KL secretariat, Jabatan Warisan Negara, Keretapi Tanah Melayu Bhd, Kuala Lumpur City Hall, Think City and local community groups.
RAC said the participation of multiple stakeholders reflected a "strong collaboration and shared commitment" towards advancing heritage preservation efforts.
Participants also joined a guided tour of the railway station, which included presentations on the building's history and architecture, completed façade restoration works and plans for the next phase of conservation.
The festival features a range of heritage and cultural activities, including traditional dance performances, busking sessions, local vendor booths, colouring competitions, health screening services and pocket talks by registered conservators involved in restoration works.
Hands-on activities such as batik printing, basket weaving, crochet crafting, traditional games and songket weaving are also being organised to encourage greater appreciation of Malaysian cultural heritage.
Built in 1910, the Kuala Lumpur Railway Station is regarded as one of the country's most significant heritage buildings due to its historical importance and distinctive architecture.
RAC said public and stakeholder programmes are part of its ongoing efforts to ensure heritage spaces remain relevant and beneficial to the community.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police arrest man over assault on food delivery rider
Article:
PETALING JAYA: Police have arrested a man over the assault of a food delivery rider during a delivery in Kelana Jaya yesterday.
Petaling Jaya deputy district police chief Superintendent M. Hussin Sollehuddin Zolkifly said the suspect, in his 30s, was detained to assist in the investigation.
"The suspect has been remanded for three days from today," he said.
It was reported that the delivery rider, who wished to be known only as Man, was allegedly assaulted by a customer while making a delivery.
Recounting the incident, Man said he accepted an order from a fast-food restaurant at a petrol station in SS6, Kelana Jaya, about 4.45am for delivery to Taman Rasa Sayang.
Upon arriving about 5.30am, he was unable to locate the address and called the customer for directions.
"I called the customer, but he didn't answer my question. He just kept asking whether I had arrived before hanging up.
"I called again, and he got angry because I was waiting at a different location... the area was dark and I couldn't see them," he said.
The case is being investigated under Sections 323 and 427 of the Penal Code.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Faris Johari accepted as 11th Undang Luak Sungei Ujong
Article:
KUALA PILAH: Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir has accepted the proclamation of Muhammad Faris Johari as the 11th Undang Luak Sungei Ujong.
The proclamation took place during a ceremony at Istana Besar Seri Menanti, where Tuanku Muhriz granted an audience to Muhammad Faris, who was selected in accordance with Sungei Ujong's customary laws.
"I am pleased to accept the proclamation of Muhammad Faris Johari as the 11th Undang Luak Sungei Ujong with the title Dato Klana Petra," Tuanku Muhriz said in his royal address.
Tuanku Muhriz said he welcomed Faris' pledge of loyalty and expected him to honour the promises he had made.
He also reminded Faris to remain loyal to the institution of the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan and to maintain close ties with the palace.
Tuanku Muhriz said relationships built on mutual respect, trust and consensus were essential to preserving the monarchy, upholding customary sovereignty and ensuring the well-being of the state.
He also called on the people of all Luaks to work closely with the customary leadership in carrying out customary affairs.
"At the same time, preserve the bonds of brotherhood and good relations with one another."
Also present were Tunku Ampuan Besar of Negri Sembilan Tuanku Aishah Rohani Tengku Besar Mahmud, Tunku Besar Seri Menanti Tunku Ali Redhauddin, Tunku Panglima Besar Negri Sembilan Tunku Zain Al-Abidin and caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun.
Muhammad Faris, an accountant, was previously selected as the 11th Dato Klana Petra, succeeding Datuk Klana Petra Datuk Mubarak Dohak after the Dewan Keadilan dan Undang accepted the latter's removal as the 10th Undang Luak Sungei Ujong.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bloomberg Malaysia strike-out bid rejected
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court has dismissed Bloomberg (Malaysia) Sdn Bhd's application to strike out a libel suit filed by Bestinet Sdn Bhd and its founder Datuk Seri Aminul Islam Abdul Nor over reports alleging migrant worker exploitation involving the company.
In his ruling on Friday, High Court judge Roslan Mat Nor said issues concerning Bloomberg Malaysia's pleadings on the article titled "Everyone Gets A Cut, And Migrant Workers Pay The Price" require further examination at trial and cannot be determined summarily based solely on affidavit evidence.
The judge said among the pleadings which need further examination were whether Bloomberg (Malaysia) was indeed involved in the actual publication of the disputed article, or if it has control over or operates the main Bloomberg website.
The court also made an order of RM5,000 of cost, according to Bestinet.
During submissions earlier on Friday, Datuk Amer Hamzah Arshad, the counsel for Bloomberg Malaysia, stressed that in any defamation action, the main consideration is publication of the impugned article or comments.
The lawyer from AmerBON Advocates argued that his clients were not involved in the publication of the impugned article in Malaysia or elsewhere.
The Bloomberg website, he argued, clearly states that Bloomberg LP - the parent company based in New York, US - operates the website and had published the article.
Therefore, he said, the defamation suit against his clients was not sustainable.
The plaintiffs' lawyer, Ravi Nekoo from Lui & Bhullar, argued that Bloomberg Malaysia was not a separate legal entity from its parent company but has some responsibility in the publication of the said article.
Ravi added that Bloomberg Malaysia's assertion that it has no control over publications and merely provided local support services for its parent company needed to be tested at the trial stage.
Besides Blooomberg LP and Bloomberg Malaysia, Bestinet and its founder are suing eight others.
They included news outlets Malaysiakini, The Edge, former Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief Latheefa Koya and Pandan Member of Parliament Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli.
The suit revolves around Bloomberg's report in January this year of Bestinet's operations of the Foreign Worker Centralised Management System, which is used to process foreign worker entry into Malaysia.
Bestinet and Aminul Islam claim the statements were defamatory, untrue and had severely damaged their reputation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls see 172 candidates, 42 multi-cornered contests
Article:
ISKANDAR PUTERI: A total of 172 candidates will contest the 56 seats in the Johor election, with 42 constituencies set to witness multi-cornered contests.
Three seats will see five-cornered contests, while 12 will feature four-cornered battles and 27 will have three-cornered fights. The remaining 14 seats will see straight fights.
Election Commission (EC) chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said all 172 nomination papers submitted at the state's 56 nomination centres were accepted, with none rejected.
The candidates comprise 56 from Barisan Nasional and Pakatan Harapan respectively, 33 from Perikatan Nasional, 15 from Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama), four from Muda, one each from Parti Orang Asli Malaysia (Asli) and Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM), as well as six independents.
Ramlan said male candidates continued to dominate the field, with 138 men and 34 women contesting.
The youngest candidate is 23-year-old PH's Danish Hossman Abd Rahman, who is contesting Johor Lama, while the oldest is 73-year-old PN candidate Lim Chin Eng in Stulang.
In terms of age, 50 candidates are aged 40-49, followed by 48 aged 50-59, 38 aged 30-39, 27 aged 60 and above, and nine aged between 21 and 29.
"Campaigning ends at 11.59pm on July 10," Ramlan told a press conference at Bangunan Sultan Ismail.
To ensure compliance with election laws, Ramlan said the EC had established 56 Election Campaign Enforcement Teams to monitor campaign activities throughout the campaign period.
He added that the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission had activated five 24-hour operations rooms across Johor from today until July 12 to receive complaints related to corruption and abuse of power during the election.
Ramlan reminded candidates that all campaign materials must comply with election laws, including displaying the names and addresses of the printer and publisher, except for party flags, party symbols and photographs of party leaders or candidates.
He also warned that campaign materials containing racial incitement, religiously sensitive content or statements that could create hostility were strictly prohibited.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Drug syndicates exploiting encrypted platforms, digital black markets, says Saifuddin
Article:
KEPALA BATAS: International drug syndicates are exploiting encrypted communication platforms, digital black markets and cross-border financial transactions to expand their trafficking networks, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said.
He said advances in digital technology had made the drug crime ecosystem increasingly complex and harder to detect.
"The evolution of digital technology has been manipulated by international syndicates to expand drug trafficking networks through encrypted communication platforms, digital black markets and cross-border financial transactions.
"This has made the drug crime ecosystem more complex, hybrid and difficult to detect, demanding a more modern, integrated and intelligence-driven enforcement approach," he said in a speech at the National Anti-Drug Day 2026 celebration today.
His speech was delivered by Home Ministry secretary-general Datuk Dr Awang Alik Jeman.
Saifuddin said Malaysia faced the same challenges as the rest of the world, with synthetic drugs increasingly replacing traditional narcotics and criminal syndicates using technology to evade detection.
He said amphetamine-type stimulants (ATS), including syabu, ecstasy and yaba pills, continued to dominate drug abuse in Malaysia, with 73,611 people, or 72.92 per cent of those detected for drug abuse and addiction as of March this year, linked to ATS.
More worrying, he said, authorities had detected growing abuse of synthetic cannabis, vape liquids laced with psychoactive substances and prescription medicines, including tramadol, codeine, diazepam, alprazolam and pregabalin, for recreational use.
"The situation is further aggravated by organised syndicates, the use of digital platforms as transaction channels and the involvement of locals in international criminal networks," he added.
Saifuddin said the government would strengthen the use of big data analytics and artificial intelligence (AI) to identify emerging trends and high-risk areas more accurately, supported by intelligence sharing among agencies and community-based intervention programmes.
"The use of AI and big data will enable more targeted, faster and higher-impact action in addressing the drug threat comprehensively," he said.
He said the government remained committed to tackling the drug menace through demand reduction, supply reduction and harm reduction, while recent amendments to the Drug Dependants (Treatment and Rehabilitation) Act 1983 had expanded access to treatment and rehabilitation.
He urged state governments, educational institutions, the private sector, non-governmental organisations and local communities to treat drug prevention as a shared national responsibility.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Synthetic drugs dominate drug abuse cases, says Saifuddin
Article:
KEPALA BATAS: Synthetic drugs now account for nearly three-quarters of drug abuse cases in Malaysia, prompting authorities to step up artificial intelligence-driven enforcement and prevention efforts.
Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said amphetamine-type stimulants (ATS), including methamphetamine (syabu), ecstasy and yaba pills, continued to dominate drug abuse trends nationwide.
He said the trend reflected a worrying shift away from traditional narcotics such as heroin and cannabis.
"As of March this year, 73,611 individuals, or 72.92 per cent of those detected for drug abuse and addiction, were involved with ATS.
"This trend underscores the increasingly complex challenges posed by synthetic drugs," he said in a speech at the National Anti-Drug Day 2026 celebration here today.
His speech was delivered by Home Ministry secretary-general Datuk Dr Awang Alik Jeman.
Saifuddin said the emergence of synthetic cannabis, psychoactive substances mixed into vape liquids, and the recreational abuse of prescription medicines such as tramadol, codeine, diazepam, alprazolam and pregabalin had further complicated enforcement and treatment efforts.
He said criminal syndicates were exploiting encrypted communications, digital marketplaces and cross-border financial transactions to expand drug trafficking networks, requiring more sophisticated intelligence-led operations.
"Responding to these evolving threats, the government is strengthening the use of big data analytics and artificial intelligence to identify emerging trends and high-risk areas more accurately, supported by intelligence sharing and community-based interventions," he added.
Saifuddin said Malaysia recorded a marginal 0.5 per cent decline in drug abusers and addicts to 191,832 people last year from 192,857 in 2024, but the figure remained alarmingly high and required sustained national commitment.
He said border states such as Kelantan, Terengganu, Perlis and Kedah continued to record the highest drug abuse rates, while Penang ranked sixth with 757 cases per 100,000 population, compared with the national average of 560.
Saifuddin said drug abuse was no longer confined to urban areas but had spread to rural communities, educational institutions and workplaces.
He said the government remained committed to tackling the drug menace through a balanced approach focused on demand reduction, supply reduction and harm reduction.
Saifuddin said amendments to the Drug Dependants (Treatment and Rehabilitation) Act 1983 had expanded access to treatment and rehabilitation, strengthening the National Anti-Drugs Agency's role in prevention, detection, treatment and recovery.
He urged political leaders, state governments, local authorities, educational institutions, the private sector, non-governmental organisations and communities to treat drug prevention as a shared national responsibility.
Malaysia has adopted "Drug Prevention is Everyone's Responsibility" as the theme for this year's National Anti-Drug Day celebration, in line with the United Nations' International Day Against Drug Abuse and Illicit Trafficking.
Saifuddin recently said authorities and regulators must fast-track the scheduling of synthetic drug chemicals under the Poisons Act 1952 to ensure legal action against offenders is not impeded.
He said
swift action
was critical, warning that delays in gazetting the substances would severely hamper police investigations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BN confident of winning back Bukit Kepong seat as Johor election campaign begin
Article:
PAGOH, June 27 — Barisan Nasional (BN) has kicked off its campaign for the 16th Johor state election with high confidence in its bid to wrest back the Bukit Kepong seat from Perikatan Nasional (PN).
BN candidate Ahmad Syar’e Yusof said the party’s confidence is fuelled by its robust divisional-level machinery, which has been steadily built over the past three years, alongside solid backing from both leadership and grassroots members.
“UMNO Pagoh and BN are in the best possible shape right now, and insya-Allah, we will carry this momentum all the way to polling day,” he told reporters after the nomination process at the Kompleks Sukan Pagoh nomination centre here today.
On facing the incumbent from Bersatu, Ahmad Syar’e said he feels no pressure; instead, he wants voters to judge for themselves the incumbent’s track record over the past two terms.
“After being given the trust and opportunity by the people of Bukit Kepong for two terms, let’s take a close look at what they have actually delivered for the people,” he said.
The former teacher also claimed that several development proposals, including housing projects that had been planned earlier, were not pursued, an issue he intends to highlight throughout the campaign period.
In this state poll, Ahmad Syar’e will face a three-cornered contest against incumbent Datuk Dr Shahruddin Jamal from PN, who is also the 17th Johor menteri besar, and C. Subramani from PH.
Bukit Kepong, which falls under the Pagoh parliamentary constituency, has 37,683 eligible voters for this state election.
The Johor state election will take place on July 11, with early voting on July 7.
For the latest updates on the 16th Johor state election, please visit https://prn.bernama.com/johor/. — Bernama

Title: Anwar announces RM1.5m allocation for Syariah legal aid, says reforms will not affect non-Muslims’ rights
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, June 27 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has announced a RM1.5 million allocation to strengthen access to Syariah legal aid services for the underprivileged, alongside renewed assurances that efforts to enhance the Syariah judicial system will not affect the rights of non-Muslims.
He said the allocation to the Syariah Lawyers Association of Malaysia would be channelled through the Syariah Legal Aid Centre (BAGUS) to expand access to representation for those unable to afford Syariah legal services, particularly the poor and single mothers.
He said access to justice remained a key concern despite the existence of a robust legal framework and capable judges and lawyers, noting that financial disparity often determined the quality of legal representation in court.
“We have good laws, good courts, good lawyers and good judges, but access remains an issue.
“Usually in the legal system, those who are well-off can afford the best lawyers, but the poorest sometimes have no access at all,” he said when officiating the 2026 National Convention of the Syariah Legal Profession here today.
Anwar also addressed ongoing discussions surrounding the strengthening of the Syariah judiciary, including proposals related to Article 121(1A) of the Federal Constitution, stressing that such efforts are not intended to undermine the rights of non-Muslims.
He said the original intent of the constitutional provision introduced in the late 1980s was to reinforce the jurisdiction of the Syariah Court in matters concerning Muslims, without interfering in the affairs of other faiths.
“We do not in any way wish to infringe upon the rights of non-Muslims. What we want to uphold is justice, and not deny non-Muslims their rights in matters of their religion.
“The civil court has no authority to interfere in the Syariah Court’s jurisdiction over Islamic law and the Muslim community. To me, that is fair,” he said.
The Prime Minister noted that he had previously assured non-Muslim leaders that amendments to Article 121(1A) would not be misinterpreted as an attempt to erode their rights.
Anwar also rejected claims that the Unity Government had neglected efforts to strengthen Islamic institutions, saying all proposals related to the Syariah judiciary had been given due consideration at Cabinet level without objection from non-Muslim ministers.
“Throughout my more than three years as Prime Minister, there has not been a single proposal related to increasing allocations or strengthening the Syariah Court that has been opposed by any minister, including non-Malay ministers,” he said.
To further enhance the Syariah judicial system, Anwar proposed a dedicated engagement session involving the Attorney General, Chief Justice, heads of Syariah courts, and representatives from the Syariah legal profession.
He said the session would allow the government to consider proposals from legal practitioners aimed at strengthening the capability and standing of the Syariah judiciary.
“I will give priority to views that are deemed necessary and important,” he said. — Bernama

Title: In Malaysia, one in four rehab patients now battling addiction and mental illness
Article:
KEPALA BATAS, June 27 — More than one in four drug addicts undergoing rehabilitation in Malaysia this year have also been diagnosed with mental health disorders, as the rise of synthetic drugs reshapes the country's addiction crisis.
National Anti-Drug Agency (AADK) director-general Datuk Ruslin Jusoh said 1,346 people, or 27 per cent of the 5,030 addicts and substance abusers undergoing treatment at Narcotics Addiction Rehabilitation Centres between January and March this year were found to have what is known as a dual diagnosis, the
New Straits Times
reported today.
He said the figure marks a sharp increase from the same period last year, when 548 people, or 6 per cent of 9,361 individuals undergoing rehabilitation, were diagnosed with both substance addiction and psychiatric disorders.
Ruslin said the trend showed drug rehabilitation was no longer just about treating addiction, as an increasing number of patients were also struggling with mental illness.
“This is no longer just about drug addiction. We are now dealing with addicts who are also suffering from mental health disorders, and this has become a major challenge because our core business has traditionally focused on treating substance dependence,” he was quoted telling reporters after the National Anti-Drug Day 2026 celebration here.
He said most of the psychiatric conditions were linked to synthetic drugs, particularly amphetamine-type stimulants such as syabu, yaba pills and ecstasy.
According to AADK, synthetic drugs now account for more than 73 per cent of all drug abuse cases in Malaysia.
The users and the mental toll
Ruslin said youths aged between 15 and 39 make up 84 per cent of synthetic drug users, with the agency continuing to see the figure climb.
He said the highest rates of synthetic drug abuse were recorded in the east coast states, where syabu and yaba pills remain the drugs of choice, although abuse is also widespread in the west coast.
National Anti-Drug Agency director-general Datuk Ruslin Jusoh (standing interacts with residents of the Kampung Selamat Narcotics Addiction Rehabilitation Centre in Tasek Gelugor, Penang on June 26, 2026. — Bernama pic
Ruslin said peer influence, easy access to drugs, their rapid intoxicating effects, and personal or family problems were among the main reasons young people turned to synthetic drugs.
He warned that some patients with dual diagnosis suffered depression, emotional instability and hallucinations, with some becoming prone to self-harm or suicide attempts.
“Some experience hallucinations severe enough to drive them towards self-harm or suicidal behaviour. This is why these cases require more specialised intervention than conventional addiction treatment,” he said.
Ruslin said the emergence of synthetic drugs, including those mixed into vape liquids, had fundamentally changed the nature of addiction in Malaysia.
He said AADK was strengthening its rehabilitation modules and working closely with the Health Ministry to provide more integrated treatment for patients with addiction and mental health disorders.
“The challenge today is very different from the past. We must continuously improve our treatment approaches because synthetic drugs have changed both the profile of addicts and the complexity of their recovery,” he was quoted as saying.

Title: Malaysia's biggest tech challenge is talent, not infrastructure, says Mosti
Article:
NIBONG TEBAL, June 27 — Malaysia’s biggest challenge in becoming a technology-creating nation is no longer infrastructure, but developing high-quality, future-ready, and sustainable local talent.
Ministry of Science, Technology and Innovation’s (MOSTI) deputy secretary-general (Planning and Science Enculturation) Datuk Ruziah Shafie said that developing a stronger, more resilient, and globally competitive future workforce requires early exposure to science, technology, and innovation (STI), as well as science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM), through continuous science culture and outreach programmes.
She said that the National Science Week (MSN) programme is one of MOSTI’s key initiatives to cultivate youth’s interest in these fields while also bridging the gap between classroom learning and the real needs of high-technology industries.
“We are all aware that today’s geopolitical landscape and global supply chain challenges require countries to achieve technology sovereignty. Malaysia can no longer remain merely a consumer of technology; we must rise to become a creator of technology.
“The greatest challenge we face now is ensuring that our local talent possesses the quality, readiness, and capability to adapt to the rapid pace of global change,” she said.
She said this while officiating the launch of the STEM Showcase held in conjunction with the MSN 2026 -Northern Zone at Sekolah Menengah Jenis Kebangsaan (SMJK) Jit Sin II today. The event was also attended by Tech Dome Penang chief executive officer Khoo Boo Wooi.
Ruziah said that when launching the MSN 2026 programme in April, Science, Technology and Innovation Minister Datuk Chang Lih Kang set a target of 1.15 million participants nationwide, with the goal of delivering inclusive and impactful exposure to STI and STEM across communities.
She said the selection of Seberang Perai Selatan (SPS) as the venue for the first MSN 2026 - Northern Zone Carnival was highly strategic, given Penang’s position as the country’s high-tech hub, particularly in the semiconductor and advanced packaging industries. The state is further supported by the technology ecosystem at Kulim Hi-Tech Park in Kedah, while Perak holds significant potential for the development of the rare earth industry.
Ruziah also said that MOSTI’s STI Foresight 2025-2040: Future of STEM Talent in Malaysia study identified 10 priority areas for the future: artificial intelligence (AI), data technologies, cybersecurity, materials science, advanced manufacturing, energy, environmental sustainability, biomedicine, intelligent systems, and digital transformation.
The study also identified key competencies that should be cultivated among future talent, including adaptability, critical thinking, digital literacy, problem-solving, creativity, communication, teamwork, ethics, and the ability to think with a future-oriented mindset.
In line with these findings, she said MOSTI’s science outreach programmes place strong emphasis on hands-on learning to foster a culture of innovation from primary through secondary school, while also encouraging the application of scientific thinking in everyday life.
Ruziah said that exposure to technologies such as AI is also essential, as the future economy will be driven by individuals who can harness technology to enhance productivity, improve efficiency, and create greater value. This, she said, will help nurture a generation that is technologically literate, creative, ethical, and responsible.
The two-day MSN 2026 - Northern Zone, organised by MOSTI’s Rakan MySains, Tech Dome Penang,  brought together a wide range of exhibitions, workshops, and interactive activities aimed at promoting a stronger culture of STI in line with the National Science, Technology and Innovation Policy (NSTIP) 2021-2030.
The programme, which concluded today, was expected to attract around 20,000 visitors and also featured a new element - nuclear advocacy - showcasing nuclear technology and its benefits to the public. — Bernama

Title: Prescription for trouble: Unlicensed doctor at Subang Bestari clinic nabbed after eight months of cut-price consultations
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 28 — A Pakistani man suspected of posing as an unlicensed doctor has been detained after Immigration officers raided a private clinic in Subang Bestari, Selangor, uncovering what authorities believe was an illegally operated medical practice that had been running for eight months.
The Immigration Department said the operation was carried out at 11.52am on June 25 by officers from its Intelligence and Special Operations Division at the Putrajaya headquarters following a month-long intelligence-gathering exercise.
The suspect is believed to have been working as an “unlicensed doctor” despite not possessing any valid pass or travel documents to remain in Malaysia, the Immigration Department said on Facebook today.
It added that this was the fourth case detected by Immigration since 2024 involving a foreign national allegedly impersonating or working as an unlicensed doctor in the country.
Officers also seized medical equipment believed to have been used in the operation, along with the suspect’s name stamp, the clinic’s official stamp, a tablet device, business transaction records, an Annual Practising Certificate (APC), and documents linked to the Companies Commission of Malaysia.
The private clinic was allegedly run by a foreign national who did not have legal authorisation to work in Malaysia’s medical sector, the department said.
The detained suspect is also believed to have been hired by that foreigner – whose nationality was not disclosed – to carry out duties as an unlicensed doctor.
The department also alleged that the clinic used another person's APC and medical qualifications to convince patients and avoid detection by the authorities.
